recommendation_rationale: BT-03 (PT private oncology center / hospital group) has the best composite under PoV constraints: it is the thesis whose required pilot is closest to what is already feasible (≥2 hospital buyers @ ≥€200/mo, EV-047), shortest time to first revenue (~6–9 months), and produces exactly the data asset (hospital deployment + GAD-7/PHQ-9 endpoints in BCS) that unlocks BT-01 (DiGA) and BT-02 (pharma) downstream. It honors the Trojan Horse: narrow BCS wedge in PT private senology, then expand to oncology survivorship cohorts and DiGA-grade dossiers.

theses.name: DiGA companion-DTx for BCS survivorship (DE first, then EU)
theses.one_line: List a Class IIa BCS-survivorship adaptive DTx in the German DiGA Verzeichnis, reimbursed €200–600/patient/year by GKV, with PerSense's AUC 0.78 depression model as the clinical claim.
theses.customer: Statutory Health Insurance funds (GKV, 73M insureds [EV-061]); secondary EU payers via DiGA-equivalent pathways (FR PECAN, BE).
theses.buyer_user_split: GKV pays; oncologist or psycho-oncologist prescribes (DiGA can be physician-prescribed); BCS patient uses.
theses.revenue_model: Per-prescription, ~90-day activation, billed annually per active patient.

price_point_hypothesis.note: DiGA reimbursement band €200–600/patient/year [EV-060]; 42% of approved DiGAs are mental health [EV-035]; HelloBetter precedent [EV-027].

tam_serviceable.value_eur: ~€26M/yr serviceable revenue at 10% penetration of EU BCS survivors
tam_serviceable.calc: 750K BCS survivors EU [EV-020] × 10% reachable via DiGA-equivalent reimbursement (DE+FR initially) × €350/yr = €26.25M. Conservative DE-only cut: ~150K BCS survivors in DE × 10% × €350 = €5.25M.

theses.defensibility: Clinical evidence (AUC 0.78 depression in BCS, N=126, 2 hospitals [EV-043]) + DiGA listing as regulatory moat + BCS-specific JITAI dataset (no competitor has BCS-cohort sensing data at this scale). HelloBetter-class barrier; takes a clone 2–3 years and €500K+ to replicate.
theses.pov_pilot_fit: Pilot must produce GAD-7/PHQ-9 endpoints + DiGA-grade data architecture (mindLAMP Hetzner GDPR [EV-038]) + audit-ready consent flow in BCS cohort. The current PoV's clinical-endpoint design is partially aligned but 8–12 weeks is too short for DiGA's required RCT-style evidence — pilot is feasibility/de-risking, not the DiGA dossier study itself. Mark as land-and-expand: BT-03 produces the seed data, BT-01 is the 24–36 mo Phase 2.
theses.wedge: BCS survivors in DE post-active-treatment, prescribed by university-hospital senology/psycho-oncology units that already engage with DiGA-listed apps (HelloBetter precedent, EV-027).
theses.expansion_path: Once the DiGA dossier proves clinical effect in BCS, expand the same DiGA listing to (a) other oncology survivorship cohorts (colorectal, gynecologic — EV-006), and (b) non-oncology chronic disease survivorship (cardiac rehab) under MDR Class IIa amendments. Adjacent buyer: French PECAN, then NL/BE payer equivalents.
theses.buyer_pain: GKV's monetizable pain: untreated post-cancer mental health drives downstream costs (recurrence-related psychosomatic GP visits, antidepressant escalation). EU mental-health burden >€100B/yr [EV-078]; Swiss Re/Wysa precedent showed -30% depression KPI for insurer [EV-036]. A €350/yr prescription that prevents one €1.5K psychiatry referral per 5 patients is positive ROI.
top_risks: MDR Class IIa cost/timeline (€300–700K, 24–36 mo) burns runway before revenue [EV-057, EV-058] — Pear Therapeutics/Mindstrong cautionary tales [EV-068, EV-069].
JITAI meta-analysis effect size is small (g=0.15 [EV-022]) — DiGA evaluation may demand stronger BCS-specific RCT.
Germany-first requires German clinical sites and DE-language adaptation; PerSense's evidence base is PT-anchored.
open_issues: AUC 0.78 [EV-043] is internal_only N=126; not yet a regulator-grade clinical claim — needs external replication.
No BCS-specific RCT in the ledger that connects JITAI effect size to BCS depression outcomes — DiGA reviewer may reject extrapolation.
DE clinical partner not yet identified; HelloBetter expansion to FR is the only adjacent precedent [EV-027].

theses.name: Pharma companion / BCS survivorship grant (Roche, Pfizer, Novartis)
theses.one_line: License PerSense as a digital companion to a pharma sponsor's BCS portfolio (e.g., Roche Kadcyla/Phesgo, Pfizer Ibrance, Novartis Kisqali) for survivorship engagement and real-world evidence on adherence + affective trajectories.
theses.customer: Pharma medical-affairs / digital-health innovation arms (Roche, Pfizer, Novartis, Lilly breast-oncology divisions).
theses.buyer_user_split: Pharma sponsor pays research-services contract; oncologist enrolls; BCS patient uses; data flows back to pharma (pseudonymized RWE on adherence, PROs, affective state).
theses.revenue_model: Multi-year research-services contract per cohort/study; €20–60K per contract milestone, scaling to €200K–€1M for multi-site registry.

price_point_hypothesis.value: €40K initial pilot contract; €250K–€1M multi-year RWE registry
price_point_hypothesis.note: Anchor: pharma research-services contracts €20–60K [EV-073]; scaled by N=200–500 BCS multi-year registry.

tam_serviceable.calc: Top-4 breast-oncology pharma × 1–2 cohort contracts/yr × €250K–€1M = €1M–€8M; conservative early-stage €3M.

regulatory_path.path: None for research-services (data processor under sponsor's protocol); MDR Class I if branded as a wellness adjunct

theses.defensibility: BCS-specific JITAI dataset + AUC 0.78 depression model [EV-043] is uniquely valuable to pharma seeking PRO/affective signals adjacent to drug adherence. Oncology is the most-funded EU digital-health segment ($810M in 2024 [EV-050]). Jasper Health raised $31.8M on a similar thesis [EV-025].
theses.pov_pilot_fit: Pilot must demonstrate (a) consent rate above the 34% iNNOVSensing baseline [EV-041], (b) data completeness ≥70% [EV-047], (c) at least one affective-state signal correlating with adherence proxies. Current PoV design is well-aligned IF a pharma observer is invited to scope.
theses.wedge: BCS survivors on adjuvant CDK4/6-inhibitor or anti-HER2 therapy in PT IPO/university hospitals — sponsor cares about adherence drop-off in months 6–24 (well-documented industry pain).
theses.expansion_path: Once one BCS pharma contract is signed, expand to (a) other tumor types in same sponsor's portfolio (lung, GI), (b) CNS pharma (Lundbeck, Otsuka) for SSRI-adherence — bridging into the broader mental-health pharma RWE market. Adjacent buyer: CRO partnerships (IQVIA, Parexel) as channel multipliers.
theses.buyer_pain: Pharma medical-affairs pain: post-launch RWE generation costs $500K–$2M per study; affective-state PROs in BCS adjuvant therapy are essentially uncollected. A €250K JITAI-based registry that produces 12 months of EMA + sensor data on N=200 is 3–5× cheaper than a CRO equivalent.
top_risks: Pharma sales cycles are 12–18 months and require named champion; PerSense has no documented pharma LOI.
Pharma may demand exclusivity that locks out BT-01 DiGA path or BT-03 hospital expansion.
Reputational risk if a pharma-funded study is perceived as marketing rather than clinical evidence — credibility for DiGA dossier could be compromised.
open_issues: No named pharma champion in the strategic report — outreach plan unwritten.
Whether PerSense's data architecture meets pharma SDTM/CDISC requirements is unverified.
Pricing anchor [EV-073] is broad; specific per-patient-month rate for BCS adherence registries not benchmarked.

theses.name: PT private oncology center / hospital group white-label (CUF, Lusíadas, Luz Saúde)
theses.one_line: Sell the PerSense BCS survivorship dashboard + JITAI app as a white-label clinical service to PT private oncology lines (CUF Oncologia, Lusíadas, Luz Saúde), priced as an annual hospital subscription, with senology/psycho-oncology directors as the buyers.
theses.customer: Private hospital group oncology service-line directors and psycho-oncology coordinators in PT (CUF, Lusíadas, Luz Saúde, Hospital da Luz).
theses.buyer_user_split: Hospital department pays subscription; senologist/oncologist refers; psycho-oncologist or specialist nurse monitors dashboard; BCS patient uses app between visits.
theses.revenue_model: Annual hospital subscription + per-clinician seat. €15–30K/year base + €100–300/clinician/month, with €5–10K setup.

price_point_hypothesis.note: White-label B2B band €15–50K/yr [EV-083]; PerSense Phase-2 dashboard €500–2K/mo [EV-084]; PoV threshold ≥2 buyers @ ≥€200/mo [EV-047].

tam_serviceable.calc: ~30 PT private + IPO oncology service lines treating BCS × ~50% addressable in 24 mo × ~€20K base + 3 clinician seats × €200/mo × 12 = ~€32K/site/yr; 15 sites × €32K = €480K/yr early; expanded to 50 sites EU-PT/ES → €1.5M+. Spain mental-health digital market €420M [EV-076] supports cross-border expansion.

regulatory_path.path: MDR Class I (clinical decision support, non-diagnostic) initially; Class IIa upgrade once expansion to DiGA

theses.defensibility: Existing 2-hospital deployment + N=126 BCS dataset + AUC 0.78 model [EV-043] = no PT competitor can demonstrate BCS-specific track record. Local language, GDPR-on-Hetzner [EV-038], existing relationships with senology/psycho-oncology PIs. SilverCloud-style hospital lock-in [EV-033]: once integrated into the survivorship pathway, switching cost is high.
theses.pov_pilot_fit: Direct fit. The PoV success metrics are explicitly written to validate this thesis: ≥2 hospitals say they would pay ≥€200/mo [EV-047]; ≥70% data completeness; ≥50% eligibility approached. Pilot cost €5–10K [EV-046]. Run at IPO + 1 private hospital BCS clinic; outcome converts directly to LOI/contract.
theses.wedge: BCS survivors in first 24 months post-active-treatment seen at PT private senology clinics (CUF Oncologia BCS line, Lusíadas mama-unit) — buyer has visible psycho-oncology capacity gap and pays a flat monthly price to extend monitoring between consultations.
theses.expansion_path: Once 5+ PT BCS sites are paying, expand on three vectors: (a) other oncology survivorship cohorts in the same hospital groups (colorectal, gynecologic — EV-006); (b) ES private hospital groups (Quirón, HM, Vithas) leveraging ES market [EV-076]; (c) the dashboard + GAD-7/PHQ-9 longitudinal dataset becomes the seed for BT-01 DiGA dossier and BT-02 pharma RWE contracts.
theses.buyer_pain: Senology/psycho-oncology director's monetizable pain: <20% of BCS receive psychological support [EV-016] yet 30–40% have clinical anxiety/depression [EV-019] — director cannot scale 1:1 psycho-oncology to the cohort and lacks visibility on between-visit deterioration. A €200/clinician/mo dashboard that flags high-risk patients (AUC 0.78 [EV-043]) avoids ~1 escalated psychiatric referral/month/clinician (~€500–1K cost externalized to family or SNS), improves retention in private oncology follow-up (revenue protection per BCS patient ~€2–5K/yr in private follow-up).
top_risks: Private hospital procurement cycles can stall (3–9 mo); mitigation: signed LOI from IPO and 1 private partner before pilot kickoff.
Preference for in-person support documented in iNNOVSensing [EV-042] — buyer may want hybrid not pure-digital.
PT-only TAM is small (~€40–60M total digital MH market [EV-074]); cross-border expansion is required, not optional.
open_issues: No signed LOI from CUF/Lusíadas/Luz Saúde yet — assumed willingness based on market structure, not validated.
Whether the €200/clinician/mo price is a ceiling or a floor in PT private hospitals is not benchmarked.
MDR Class I scope is defensible only if the JITAI is positioned as monitoring, not diagnostic — line is fuzzy and may be challenged by Notified Body.

theses.name: IPO / public oncology centers via institutional collaboration + EU-funded research
theses.one_line: Anchor PerSense in IPO Lisboa/Porto/Coimbra and university hospital BCS survivorship programs as the institutional digital-survivorship layer, funded by EU/PT non-dilutive (COMPETE 2030, EIC, Horizon, FCT) — research-services revenue + platform-licensing once mature.
theses.customer: IPO institutional research/clinical leadership; university hospital senology departments; co-funded by COMPETE 2030 / EIC / Horizon EU consortia.
theses.buyer_user_split: Public hospital signs research-collaboration agreement; PI is the formal buyer; grant funds underwrite revenue; BCS patients enrolled via standard-of-care.
theses.revenue_model: Grant-funded research-services contracts (€80–300K per project); transition to per-site licensing fee (~€10–20K/yr) post-grant.

price_point_hypothesis.note: PT validation studies €80–150K vs €300K NHS [EV-080]; PerSense FCT €300K precedent [EV-044]; COMPETE 2030 €3.9B pool [EV-066].

tam_serviceable.calc: 5–8 IPO/university hospital sites × €150K avg multi-year contract amortized = ~€500K–€1.2M/yr; plus 1–2 EU consortium memberships at €200–500K each.

regulatory_path.path: Research-grade (no MDR for non-product use); MDR Class I for any clinical-deployment expansion

theses.defensibility: FCT €300K [EV-044] + PRODROMUS €300K FLAD [EV-045] precedents show grant-stacking competence. PT cost advantage (validation €80–150K vs NHS €300K [EV-080]) creates a structural moat for EU consortia seeking lean BCS sites. Existing 2-hospital footprint [EV-043] makes IPO/university expansion credible.
theses.pov_pilot_fit: PoV doubles as feasibility data for the next FCT/COMPETE/EIC grant. Pilot cost €5–10K [EV-046] vs iNNOVSensing €120K [EV-048] is a strong pitch for grant reviewers. Alignment is good but slightly tangential — pilot is optimized for buyer-LOI evidence, not grant-narrative evidence.
theses.wedge: BCS survivors in IPO Lisboa/Porto/Coimbra senology/onco-psychology programs — institutional cohort with 36K PT BCS [EV-020] gives a defensible PT-centric grant story.
theses.expansion_path: Once 1–2 EU consortium grants land, expand to (a) other oncology cohorts within the same IPO institutional access; (b) Horizon EU multi-country BCS consortium with DE/ES/FR partners (de facto regulatory soft-launch for BT-01 DiGA); (c) pharma sponsorship layered onto a grant-funded registry (BT-02 hybrid).
theses.buyer_pain: IPO institutional pain: research output and grant capture are KPIs; psycho-oncology has poor visibility despite being a ministry priority. PerSense provides a turnkey BCS digital-survivorship cohort that makes the PI competitive for FCT/EIC. Monetizable: a €300K FCT win = ~€100K to PerSense, ~€200K to PI/institution.
top_risks: Grant cycles are non-recurring revenue and lumpy — can't sustain a 4-FTE team alone.
EIC Accelerator success rate ~5.9% [EV-063]; can't rely on any single application.
IPO procurement and IRB cycles are slow (6–12 mo); thesis hides behind grant-funded research mode rather than product-market fit.
open_issues: Conversion path from grant-funded research to recurring license is unproven — many academic-spinout DTx fail at this transition.
No EU consortium partner identified; HelloBetter's FR expansion [EV-027] is the kind of partner needed.
Whether IPO will pay for a license post-grant or expect the tool gratis as an academic deliverable is unanswered.

theses.name: Insurer survivorship benefit (Médis, Multicare oncology riders)
theses.one_line: Embed PerSense as a survivorship mental-health benefit in PT private health insurance oncology riders (Médis Oncologia, Multicare, AdvanceCare), priced per-life-covered, justified by avoided downstream MH claims.
theses.customer: PT private health insurers (Médis/Ageas, Multicare/Fidelidade, AdvanceCare/Generali) and reinsurer co-packagers (Swiss Re).
theses.buyer_user_split: Insurer pays per-life-covered fee; insured BCS patient activates app via app-store + insurer code; oncologist optional touchpoint.
theses.revenue_model: Per-member-per-month (PMPM) for activated BCS-survivor lives, €5–15/user/mo, plus a fixed program fee €20–50K/yr.

price_point_hypothesis.note: Per-user €5–15/mo [EV-083]; Swiss Re/Wysa precedent showed -30% depression KPI [EV-036]; 40% of new MH partnerships now insurer-led [EV-072].

tam_serviceable.calc: PT private insurers cover ~3M lives; ~5K BCS survivors among insured × 30% activation × €8/mo × 12 = ~€144K/yr per insurer; 3 insurers × scaled 3–5× over 36 mo = €1.5–2M.

regulatory_path.path: MDR Class I (wellness benefit framing); Class IIa if clinical claim escalates

theses.defensibility: Insurer contracts include lock-in via integration with their member portal/claims system. AUC 0.78 model [EV-043] is the actuarial pitch (predict who'll escalate). Wysa/Swiss Re precedent [EV-036] is the public proof. Limbic AI's NHS 33% adoption [EV-026] shows mediated channel works.
theses.pov_pilot_fit: PoV would need at least one insurer observer + a defined claims-cost-avoided model; current PoV is hospital-buyer focused so alignment is partial. An insurer-track variant of the PoV would diverge — recommend keeping BT-05 as Phase-2 expansion, not the wedge.
theses.wedge: BCS survivors covered by Médis Oncologia rider in first 24 months post-active-treatment — narrow, identifiable, and the rider already contemplates psychological support but underdelivers it.
theses.expansion_path: Once one PT insurer signs, expand to (a) Spanish insurers (Sanitas, Adeslas) — €420M ES MH market [EV-076]; (b) other covered oncology cohorts; (c) employer wellness programs leveraging the insurer channel (Unmind-style [EV-071]).
theses.buyer_pain: Insurer pain: BCS rider holders escalate to high-cost MH claims (€2–5K/yr per case in PT private psych); 30–40% prevalence [EV-019] × 5K BCS insured × €3K avoided escalation in 20% of cases = €3M actuarial savings per insurer pool. A €30K + €8 PMPM program is breakeven at ~30 escalations avoided.
top_risks: PT insurer market is small and conservative; sales cycles 12+ months with no documented PerSense relationship.
Actuarial savings claim depends on a quasi-experimental study showing claims reduction — not yet generated.
PMPM model requires high activation; iNNOVSensing's 34% consent [EV-041] is below typical insurer activation thresholds and would compress unit economics.
open_issues: No insurer LOI or even named champion in the strategic report.
Whether claims-cost-avoidance can be measured in PT given fragmented MH-claims data is unverified.
Activation/consent uplift from insurer-mediated onboarding (vs research consent baseline) is unknown for BCS specifically.
